Class SerializedUpdate

java.lang.Object
it.polimi.Network.Common.SerializedUpdate
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class SerializedUpdate extends Object implements Serializable
Represents a structured update or notification sent from the server to the client.

This class acts as the standard data transfer object (DTO) for server-to-client communication, complementing ActionMessage. It encapsulates the outcome of user actions, global lobby notifications, and synchronized game state updates.

See Also:

  • Constructor Details

    • SerializedUpdate

      public SerializedUpdate()
      Default constructor. Required by the Jackson library for JSON deserialization.

    • SerializedUpdate

      public SerializedUpdate(String type, String content, boolean success)
      Constructs a new SerializedUpdate with the specified parameters.
      Parameters:
      type - The specific type of update being sent.
      content - The descriptive payload or state data.
      success - true if the update relates to a successful server operation, false otherwise.

  • Method Details

    • getType

      public String getType()
      Gets the type of the update.
      Returns:
      The update type string.

    • setType

      public void setType(String type)
      Sets the type of the update.
      Parameters:
      type - The update type string.

    • getContent

      public String getContent()
      Gets the textual content or payload of the update.
      Returns:
      The message or serialized state.

    • setContent

      public void setContent(String content)
      Sets the textual content or payload of the update.
      Parameters:
      content - The message or serialized state.

    • isSuccess

      public boolean isSuccess()
      Checks if the update denotes a successful operation.
      Returns:
      true if successful, false if it represents an error or denial.

    • setSuccess

      public void setSuccess(boolean success)
      Sets the success flag of the update.
      Parameters:
      success - true for a successful operation, false otherwise.
